requirement: Identify the study as developing and/or validating a multivariable prediction model, type of prediction (diagnosis/prognosis) and population.
evidence: Title and abstract identify prognosis prediction of haemodynamic instability (hypotension/hypertension/variability) in ICU patients.

requirement: Explain the medical context (including whether diagnostic or prognostic) and rationale for developing/validating the model.
evidence: Section 1 describes clinical motivation: real-time prediction of vasopressor-induced haemodynamic events using arterial waveform beat-by-beat features.

requirement: Specify the objectives, including whether the study describes the development or validation of the model, or both.
evidence: Section 1 specifies: Act 1 development (Clínic), Acts 2–3 external validation (MIMIC-IV, VitalDB).

requirement: Describe the study design or source of data (e.g. randomised trial, cohort or registry data), separately for the development and validation data sets.
evidence: Section 2: retrospective observational; Clínic UCIQ (ICU), MIMIC-IV Waveform (open), VitalDB (open).

requirement: Specify the key dates, including start and end of accrual; if applicable, follow-up length.
evidence: Table 1 provides date ranges per cohort; see act5/table1_all_cohorts.csv.

requirement: Specify inclusion and exclusion criteria.
evidence: Section 2: inclusion — arterial line with ABP signal ≥30 min; exclusion — signal quality < threshold (SQIABP < 0.85 in >50% of beats).

requirement: Give details of treatments received, if relevant.
evidence: Vasopressor administration documented as context for hypertension events; full pharmacological detail in source datasets.

requirement: Clearly define the outcome that is predicted by the prediction model.
evidence: Section 3.2: hypotension = MAP < 65 mmHg for ≥5 min; hypertension = MAP > 110 mmHg for ≥5 min; variability = IQR of MAP > 20 mmHg per 30 min window.

requirement: Report any actions to blind assessment of the outcome, if applicable.
evidence: External cohorts (MIMIC, VitalDB) were processed with identical automated pipeline; no manual adjudication — blinding not applicable.

requirement: Clearly define all predictors used in developing the model.
evidence: Section 3.3: 8 physiological signals (BRS, SDNN, RMSSD, pNN50, ARV, CV_PA, std_PA, RSA) × 5 temporal statistics = 40 features.

requirement: Report any actions to blind assessment of predictors for the outcome, if applicable.
evidence: Features are computed from pre-event windows exclusively; label assignment does not feed back into feature computation.

requirement: Explain how the study size was arrived at.
evidence: Sample size constrained by available ICU recordings. EPV analysis (clinic development set): hypotension: 66 events / 40 features = EPV 1.65 [LOW EPV (<10)]; hypertension: 38 events / 40 features = EPV 0.95 [LOW EPV (<10)]; variability: 1596 events / 40 features = EPV 39.9 [OK]. Hypotension and hypertension EPV < 10; this is a limitation addressed in Discussion Section 6.1 (regularisation via BayesMixedGLM partial pooling; coefficient uncertainty CIs reported). Variability EPV adequate (>10).

requirement: Describe how missing data were handled.
evidence: Section 3.1: windows with >20% missing beats excluded; remaining feature NaNs imputed via forward-fill in signal pre-processing.

requirement: Describe how predictors were handled in the analysis.
evidence: Section 3.4: standardised (z-score) for GLMM; tree models use raw values.

requirement: Specify type of model, all model-building procedures (coding, selection, predictor combination).
evidence: Section 4: GLMM (penalised mixed logistic), MILP optimal tree (depth=2, MI-selected top-3 features), RF (n=300, patient-level CV), XGBoost.

requirement: Specify measures used to assess model performance and uncertainty.
evidence: AUC (95% CI from CV fold AUCs or bootstrap), Brier score, calibration-in-the-large, calibration slope, DCA net benefit.

requirement: Describe any model updating arising from the validation.
evidence: Section 5.2: CITL intercept recalibration tested for MIMIC validation; see act2_results.json glmm_auc_recalibrated.

requirement: Describe the flow of participants (development vs validation).
evidence: Table 1 (act5/table1_all_cohorts.csv) shows n_patients, n_windows, prevalence per cohort × event_type.

requirement: Present the full prediction model (point estimates, CIs).
evidence: act1_results.json: glmm_cv_auc_mean ± ci95, rf_cv_auc_ci95, xgb_cv_auc_ci95, milp_bootstrap_auc_mean.  Hypotension GLMM AUC=0.750.

requirement: Report performance measures for the model in both development and validation data sets.
evidence: Act 1: CV AUC + 95% CI.  Act 2: external AUC + bootstrap CI, CITL, slope.  Act 3: VitalDB revalidation (pending).  MIMIC GLMM hypotension AUC=0.6381142098273573.

requirement: Discuss limitations of the study, including potential bias and imprecision.
evidence: To be written: retrospective design, single-institution development, vasopressor protocol variability, dataset size constraints.

requirement: Discuss implications for clinical use of the model.
evidence: To be written: BRS and PAP variability features as real-time monitoring targets; alert system integration.
